title: INTEGER PRIMARY KEY WITHOUT ROWID virtual table crash
description:
drh added on 2018-09-24 14:10:01:
Calling sqlite3_declare_vtab() with schema that includes both
INTEGER PRIMARY KEY and WITHOUT ROWID causes an assertion fault.
The problem does not arise if the virtual table is INT PRIMARY KEY
or if the WITHOUT ROWID is omitted.  Because of these simple work-arounds
and because the problem normally requires C-level API calls, it is not
considered serious.
This problem was introduced by check-in [c601d128ff1f1dd6d221ee9f55595a95b]
on 2017-08-10, which is the check-in that added the ability for WITHOUT
ROWID virtual tables to be writable.  The first release containing this
problem was version 3.21.0 on 2017-10-24.
This problem was originally reported by an identified post on the
sqlite-users mailing list.
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE test_vtab (id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY, name TEXT NOT NULL);
INSERT INTO test_vtab (id, name) VALUES (2, NULL);
SELECT * FROM test_vtab WHERE id = (SELECT MAX(id) FROM test_vtab);
CREATE TABLE another_table (id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY, value BYTEA);
key_bug_features: INTEGER PRIMARY KEY with WITHOUT ROWID causes assertion fault
